intro.title: About Us
intro.description: Mobile Programming Club (MPC) is one of the most dynamic and creative clubs at the Faculty of Information Technology – Ho Chi Minh City Open University. MPC was established to create an environment for learning, researching and developing IT skills across web, mobile, and more.

benefits.title: Benefits of Joining
benefits.subtitle: Join MPC to grow together, learn from each other, and make a difference

skills.title: Skill Development
skills.desc: Learn and practice the latest technologies in Web, App, and AI through workshops and real projects.

network.title: Network Building
network.desc: Meet and work with like-minded people. Build lasting relationships in the tech community.

opportunity.title: Opportunities
opportunity.desc: Participate in real projects, programming contests, and tech events. Gain experience for your career.

community.title: Friendly Community
community.desc: A positive learning environment that supports and is always ready to help new members.

team.title: Club Organization
team.subtitle: Our passionate and dedicated leadership team

activities.title: Featured Activities
activities.subtitle: Signature events and projects from MPC Club

items.a: MPC (Mobile Programming Club) is the Mobile Programming Club at the Faculty of Information Technology – Ho Chi Minh City Open University, established to create a tech learning environment for students.

items.q: Do I need programming skills to join?
items.a: Not required! The club welcomes all students passionate about technology, from beginners to experienced. The most important thing is a willingness to learn and enthusiasm.
The club organizes programming skill workshops, tech seminars, competitions like Web Design and Robocode, and monthly teambuilding activities, plus real projects for members to practice.
Follow MPC's Facebook fanpage for updates on membership recruitment each semester. When recruitment opens, you can register via online form and participate in an interview.

items.q: What departments does the club have?
items.a: MPC Club has several functional departments: Programming, Events & Logistics, Communications, Academic... Each handles different activities and you can choose the one that fits your interests.

items.q: Can students from other faculties join?
items.a: The club prioritizes IT Faculty students but warmly welcomes students from other faculties who are passionate about programming and technology.

events.title: Events
events.subtitle: The club regularly hosts events, large and small, organized to bond club members and expand networking outside the club. These events also serve as entertainment after school and club hours.

internal.title: Internal Club Activities
internal.desc: The club often organizes internal activities and events such as: offline meetings, birthdays, study groups,... aiming to bond members and create a comfortable learning/working environment.

external.title: External Club Events
external.desc: Organizing university events and academic competitions like WebDesign, RoboCode,... expanding relationships with other clubs and providing opportunities for self-expression.

internalEvents.title: Offline Meetings
internalEvents.desc: A gathering for club members at a specific location to meet, talk, socialize, share knowledge and experiences, and build relationships with each other.

internalEvents.title: Birthdays
internalEvents.desc: The club annually celebrates its birthday, an occasion for everyone to meet, congratulate, and share joy together.

internalEvents.title: Event Organization
internalEvents.desc: Club members often support each other in organizing events, helping everyone understand the event organization process better and creating opportunities for members to get closer.

internalEvents.title: Study Groups
internalEvents.desc: The club frequently organizes study groups for members to learn and exchange knowledge and experiences together, helping everyone understand subjects better and creating a comfortable, effective learning environment.

externalEvents.title: WebDesign
externalEvents.desc: Web Design is an annual website interface programming competition, providing participants with a meaningful academic playground.

externalEvents.title: Robocode
externalEvents.desc: Aims to foster specialized knowledge, soft skills, and promote students creativity.

externalEvents.title: Freshman Welcome Activities
externalEvents.desc: This activity helps orient Freshmen, helping them quickly integrate, understand the university environment, and the roles of the Youth Union - Student Association - Clubs.

externalEvents.title: Knowledge Sharing Sessions
externalEvents.desc: Organizing sessions to share knowledge and experiences from club members, as well as industry experts.

externalEvents.title: The Open Clubs
externalEvents.desc: An exchange, performance, and club introduction event for all university students organized by the University Youth Union.

webdesign.title:
Academic Competition 
Web Design
webdesign.subtitle: Web Design is an annual website interface programming competition organized by the Mobile Programming Club with the desire to bring a meaningful and creative playground.
webdesign.heading: Web Design Competition
webdesign.purposeDesc: Web Design was created to provide a healthy academic playground, helping students apply their learned knowledge into practice. Thereby helping competing teams develop teamwork skills, time management skills, UI/UX design, and Frontend programming. The competition emphasizes not only programming skills but also the usability and aesthetics of the product.
webdesign.rules1Desc: Contestants register to form teams of up to 3 members
webdesign.rules2Desc: The representative contestant fills out the organizing committees registration form
webdesign.rules3Desc: Audiences follow and participate in mini-games at the finale
webdesign.phase1Desc: Open registration form, announce competition theme
webdesign.phase2Desc: A series of media posts regarding rules and prize structure
webdesign.phase3Desc: The process of finalizing and submitting projects by teams
webdesign.phase4Desc: Top scoring teams will present their products directly to the jury
webdesign.criteria1Desc: Transparent layout, harmonious colors, responsive design
webdesign.criteria2Desc: Easy to use, convenient operations, fast loading
webdesign.criteria4Desc: Fits the competition theme, practical and usable
webdesign.prize1Desc: Worth more than 1.000.000 VNĐ, special gifts from sponsors and certificates
webdesign.prize2Desc: Worth more than 700.000 VNĐ, special gifts from sponsors and certificates
webdesign.prize3Desc: Worth more than 500.000 VNĐ, special gifts from sponsors and certificates
webdesign.joinDesc: See more details on our fanpage for additional information about the rules, prize structure, and how to register to participate.
